Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate owned filenames, confine paths, reject traversal and unsafe archive entries, and handle collisions and Unicode deliberately.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Path Archive Defense model · Theory Path Archive…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Removing ../ makes an untrusted path safe.
